description: Evergreen topic bank for the Visage Aesthetics auto-blog generator. The script picks one topic per run that (a) hasn't been covered yet, (b) has high search intent, and (c) ideally connects to a recent news angle from web search.

evergreenTopics.title: Lip filler aftercare: a complete guide
targetKeywords: what to do after lip filler
evergreenTopics.angle: Calm walkthrough of the first 48 hours, the 2-week settle, what to avoid, when to worry. Bernadette's checklist.

evergreenTopics.title: How long does Botox actually last?
evergreenTopics.angle: 3-4 month answer, plus the variables (dose, area, metabolism, frequency of use) that move the timeline.

evergreenTopics.title: Five mistakes first-time Botox clients make
evergreenTopics.angle: Practical: too much, too soon, wrong clinic, no consultation, no review. Honest.

evergreenTopics.title: Profhilo: a no-nonsense treatment guide
evergreenTopics.angle: What it is, what it isn't, who it works on, who it doesn't. Why a course of two.

evergreenTopics.title: Anti-wrinkle aftercare: the first 24 hours
evergreenTopics.angle: Hour-by-hour. Don't lie down, don't massage, don't exercise hard. Why each rule.

evergreenTopics.title: How to find a natural-looking aesthetics clinic in Essex
evergreenTopics.angle: What to ask in a consultation, what red flags to watch for, why nurse-led matters.

evergreenTopics.title: UK aesthetics regulation in 2026: what's actually changing
evergreenTopics.angle: Latest legislative state of play, what it means for clients, why it matters who you see.

evergreenTopics.title: AQUALYX fat dissolving: what to expect
evergreenTopics.angle: Mechanism, candidate selection, course planning, downtime, realistic results.

evergreenTopics.title: Tear trough filler: when it works and when it really doesn't
evergreenTopics.angle: The hardest area in aesthetics. Why most clients are not candidates, and what to do instead.

evergreenTopics.title: Preventative Botox in your late twenties: yes or no
evergreenTopics.angle: An honest answer for the under-30 demographic. When it makes sense, when it doesn't, what to start with.

evergreenTopics.title: Aesthetic treatment for men: what's different
evergreenTopics.angle: Different anatomy, different dosing, different goals. Subtle is even more important.

evergreenTopics.title: Ten questions to ask at any aesthetics consultation
evergreenTopics.angle: Question 1: NMC pin? Question 2: indemnity insurer? Question 3: reversal protocols on site?

evergreenTopics.title: Micro-needling: when you'll see results, and what to expect
evergreenTopics.angle: Collagen takes time. 4-6 weeks per session, courses of 3, real-life expectations.

evergreenTopics.title: Botox for excessive sweating: a medical use of an aesthetic treatment
evergreenTopics.angle: Mechanism (acetylcholine block), longevity (6+ months), why this works medically.

evergreenTopics.title: HarmonyCa vs traditional dermal filler: when to choose which
evergreenTopics.angle: Hybrid HA + CaHA, biostimulation, who's the right candidate.

evergreenTopics.title: Vitamin B12 injections: when they actually work
evergreenTopics.angle: The science of injectable vs oral, who genuinely benefits, who's wasting money.

evergreenTopics.title: Exercise after Botox: how it affects your results
evergreenTopics.angle: The 24-48 hour rule and why it exists (raised heart rate, blood flow at the injection site, theoretical migration of toxin). Specific guidance on when to resume light walking, weights, HIIT, hot yoga and swimming. Whether high-frequency cardio shortens Botox duration over time, with the actual evidence. Practical timeline rather than vague reassurance.

evergreenTopics.title: Masseter Botox: jawline slimming, bruxism and TMJ relief
evergreenTopics.angle: What it does (relaxes the chewing muscle), what changes over a course (visible slimming around month 3, bruxism relief within weeks), who's a candidate, who isn't (already slim lower face). Honest on cost, course length, and the fact that some clients see no slimming because their masseter was never hypertrophied.

evergreenTopics.title: Lip flip vs lip filler: when each one is actually right
evergreenTopics.angle: A lip flip is 2-4 units of Botox into the upper lip border, lasting 6-8 weeks, giving the illusion of a fuller upper lip without volume. Filler adds actual volume for 6-12 months. When a flip is the right answer (mild gummy smile, thin vermillion border, wanting a softer first step), when filler genuinely is.

evergreenTopics.title: A chemical brow lift with Botox: what it can and can't do
evergreenTopics.angle: Strategic injection of the depressor muscles (orbicularis, procerus, corrugators) lifts the tail of the brow by 2-3mm. Why this works for early hooding, why it does nothing for severe ptosis. Where the limit is and when a surgical referral is the honest answer.

evergreenTopics.title: Gummy smile: how a small dose of Botox changes it
evergreenTopics.angle: 1-2 units into the levator labii on each side relaxes the upper lip lift. Result: gum show reduces from 4mm to 1-2mm. Why dose precision matters (over-treatment flattens the smile), what 'too much' looks like, why this is a poor choice for first-time Botox clients.

evergreenTopics.title: Neck bands and Botox: what works on the platysma
evergreenTopics.angle: Vertical neck cords (platysmal bands) respond to small doses along the band itself; horizontal neck lines respond less reliably. Where Botox is the right tool, where skin laxity means it isn't, and when the honest answer is Profhilo, micro-needling, or a referral.

evergreenTopics.title: Chin filler for jawline projection: a small change with big effect
evergreenTopics.angle: How 1-2ml in the chin can rebalance a face by improving lower-third projection and side-profile harmony. Why this often makes a nose look smaller without touching the nose. Candidate selection, longevity (12-18 months), and the trap of chasing a 'snatched' jawline.

evergreenTopics.title: Nasolabial folds: filler is rarely the right first step
evergreenTopics.angle: The fold itself is rarely the problem. Mid-face volume loss is. Why direct filler into the fold often looks worse, what supporting the cheek does instead, when a skin booster like Profhilo is the right answer, and when nothing should be done at all.

evergreenTopics.title: Marionette lines: what helps and what doesn't
evergreenTopics.angle: DAO Botox to lift the mouth corners, small amounts of filler to support the line itself, biostimulators for skin quality. Why direct filler often deepens the shadow rather than softens it. Combined approach is usually right.

evergreenTopics.title: Dissolving filler: when it's the right call
evergreenTopics.angle: Hyaluronidase enzyme dissolves HA filler within hours. When it's medically essential (vascular occlusion, infection), when it's elective (migration, over-treatment, wanting a reset), why it should only be done by a clinician who carries reversal stock on site, the test patch rule, and what happens to your natural tissue afterwards.

evergreenTopics.title: Migrated lip filler: what it looks like and why it happens
evergreenTopics.angle: Visible signs (a defined ridge above the lip border, swelling that never goes down, lips losing their natural shape), why it happens (technique, product choice, over-volumising), why MRI imaging shows long-term migration in some cases. What to do (dissolve, wait, restart with a fresh approach).

evergreenTopics.title: Ozempic face: aesthetic options for rapid weight-loss volume loss
evergreenTopics.angle: Why fast weight loss hollows the face (fat is structural, not just storage). Sensible sequencing: stabilise weight first, then assess. What restores volume responsibly (mid-face filler, skin boosters for laxity, biostimulators for collagen) versus what overcorrects. The trap of treating during active weight loss.

evergreenTopics.title: Polynucleotides vs Profhilo: two skin boosters, different jobs
evergreenTopics.angle: Profhilo (high-low MW hyaluronic acid) hydrates and biostimulates broadly. Polynucleotides (purified salmon DNA fragments) target tissue repair, including thin under-eye skin and acne scars. When each is the right tool, when neither is, and the honest state of polynucleotide regulation in the UK in 2026.

evergreenTopics.title: Profhilo for the neck and décolletage: realistic expectations
evergreenTopics.angle: The neck shows ageing faster than the face. How Profhilo's injection points on the neck differ from the face, why two sessions four weeks apart is non-negotiable, what it improves (crepiness, hydration, mild laxity), what it doesn't (deep horizontal lines, established platysmal bands, surgical-grade laxity).

evergreenTopics.title: Skin quality vs volume: how aesthetics has changed
evergreenTopics.angle: The shift away from volumising everything towards treating skin as a tissue. Why a face with great skin and no filler often looks younger than a filled face with neglected skin. How treatment plans now lead with boosters, biostimulators and micro-needling, with filler used last and sparingly.

evergreenTopics.title: Combining Botox and skin boosters: sequencing and spacing
evergreenTopics.angle: Why these two pair well (different mechanisms, different timelines), how to sequence in a single session safely, when to space across two visits, what to expect from a 12-month treatment plan that uses both rather than chasing one.

evergreenTopics.title: Micro-needling for acne scarring: what's realistic
evergreenTopics.angle: Which acne scar types respond (rolling, shallow boxcar) and which don't (deep ice-pick). Why a course of 4-6 sessions over 4-6 months is the realistic timeline. Honest percentage-improvement expectations. Why combining with polynucleotides or topical retinoids amplifies results.

evergreenTopics.title: Micro-needling vs laser resurfacing: who chooses what
evergreenTopics.angle: Mechanism differences (mechanical wound vs thermal injury), downtime (1-2 days vs 5-10), suitability by skin tone (laser is harder on darker skin), cost per course, and the specific cases where one is clearly the better answer.

evergreenTopics.title: HarmonyCa for jawline restoration: hybrid filler with collagen build
evergreenTopics.angle: How the hyaluronic acid lifts immediately while the calcium hydroxyapatite microspheres stimulate collagen over 3-6 months. Why this suits mid-life jawline laxity better than HA filler alone. Course planning (often a single session, sometimes two), longevity (12-18 months with skin-quality dividend beyond).

evergreenTopics.title: Biostimulators explained: HarmonyCa, Sculptra, Radiesse
evergreenTopics.angle: The category as a whole. How calcium hydroxyapatite and poly-L-lactic acid trigger collagen production over months rather than adding volume on the day. Who they suit (mid-30s upwards with laxity), who they don't (anyone wanting a same-day result), and how Visage uses HarmonyCa specifically.

evergreenTopics.title: CryoPen for skin tags: what to expect on the day
evergreenTopics.angle: How precision freezing destroys the lesion without damaging surrounding tissue. What the treatment feels like (a brief sting), what the area looks like over 7-14 days (small scab, then nothing), when one session is enough, when a second is needed. Why this beats home freezing kits and string-tying.

evergreenTopics.title: Removing age spots and pigmentation with CryoPen
evergreenTopics.angle: Solar lentigines on hands, décolletage and face respond well to short cryo bursts. The healing timeline (pink for a week, then settled), why suncare for the next 4-6 weeks is non-negotiable, what doesn't respond (melasma, post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation), and the right referral when it isn't a candidate lesion.

evergreenTopics.title: Milia: why they appear and how to remove them properly
evergreenTopics.angle: Tiny keratin cysts, usually around the eyes, that don't respond to squeezing or exfoliation. Why CryoPen or sterile lance-and-extract is the proper removal. What causes recurrence (heavy occlusive skincare around the eyes), how to break the cycle.

evergreenTopics.title: When to worry about a mole: the ABCDE check
evergreenTopics.angle: The standard ABCDE criteria (Asymmetry, Border, Colour, Diameter, Evolution) explained with the kind of detail patients actually need. What a normal mole looks like over time, what changes mean a clinical opinion is needed, why monthly self-checks plus annual mapping is the standard of care.

evergreenTopics.title: Mole mapping: who needs it, how often, and what the appointment involves
evergreenTopics.angle: Photographic baseline of every mole, then comparison at follow-up. Who genuinely benefits (50+ moles, family history of melanoma, fair skin with significant sun exposure, immunosuppressed), how often (annually is the baseline, six-monthly for higher-risk profiles), what the appointment actually looks like.

evergreenTopics.title: Aesthetics before your wedding: a sensible 12-month timeline
evergreenTopics.angle: 12 months out: start skin quality work (micro-needling, Profhilo). 6 months: any biostimulators. 3 months: confirm filler placement. 4 weeks before: final Botox top-up, no new treatments after. 2 weeks: no facials, no peels, no new product trials. The rule of 'no firsts close to the day'.

evergreenTopics.title: Pregnancy, breastfeeding and aesthetic treatments
evergreenTopics.angle: The honest answer on Botox (not advised, no safety data), filler (not advised), Profhilo (not advised), micro-needling (paused), skincare (retinoids out, vitamin C and SPF in). When to stop before trying to conceive, when to resume after birth and breastfeeding. Not a moral position, a safety one.

evergreenTopics.title: Vascular occlusion: the rare but serious filler complication
evergreenTopics.angle: What it is (filler blocking a blood vessel), why it's the reason hyaluronidase must be on the premises before any filler is injected, the early signs (immediate blanching, disproportionate pain, mottled skin), what a clinician should do in the first 60 minutes, why this is the question to ask in any consultation.

evergreenTopics.title: When to walk out of an aesthetics consultation
evergreenTopics.angle: Specific red flags: no NMC or GMC number visible, no medical history form, pressure to book same-day, no reversal protocols, dosage decisions before the consultation is over, a price-list-first conversation. The walking-out conversation itself, said politely.

newsAngles: MHRA / regulatory updates affecting aesthetic practice
Save Face / JCCP / BACN industry announcements
New product launches in UK aesthetic injectables
Reported complications or industry incidents prompting consumer education
Government licensing scheme rollout for non-surgical cosmetic procedures
Notable celebrity / public conversation shifting consumer awareness
Seasonal trends (wedding season, summer skin prep, winter recovery, NYE)
voiceRules: First-person from Bernadette where appropriate; British English spelling
Calm, considered, never breathy or salesy. No exclamation marks except in quotes.
Concrete numbers and timeframes over vague reassurance
Acknowledge industry weaknesses honestly (unregulated, over-treatment, etc.)
Internal links to /treatments/[relevant] and /about/qualifications where natural
End with a free-consultation CTA, never an aggressive book-now
